object.value: personal data

paragraph_text: (e) processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;
parent_texts: 1. Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:

classification_rationale: This paragraph grants permission for processing special categories of personal data under specific conditions related to professional secrecy.

paragraph_text: 3. Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.

object.value: the information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14
standardised icons

paragraph_text: 7. The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing. Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.

object.value: a copy of the personal data undergoing processing

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on the controller regarding data provision.
anchor: The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing.
paragraph_text: 3. The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.

parent_texts: For the purposes of this Regulation, the following definitions apply:

paragraph_text: (35) ‘biometric identification’ means the automated recognition of physical, physiological, behavioural, or psychological human features for the purpose of establishing the identity of a natural person by comparing biometric data of that individual to biometric data of individuals stored in a database;
parent_texts: For the purposes of this Regulation, the following definitions apply:

object.value: the registration obligations referred to in Article 49

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on specific deployers to comply with registration requirements.
anchor: Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49.
paragraph_text: 8. Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49. When such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71, they shall not use that system and shall inform the provider or the distributor.

In the case referred to in Article 46(1), deployers may be exempt from that obligation to notify.

predicate.value: ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system

object.value: AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on providers regarding the design and development of AI systems.
anchor: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system
span_coverage_truncated: observant circumspect taking account circumstances context use
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence.

object.value: that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system

condition.value: AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence

classification_rationale: This statement provides an exemption from the transparency obligation for specific AI systems.
anchor: This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences
span_coverage_truncated: observant circumspect taking account circumstances context use
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence.

object.value: the Commission

condition.value: Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a)

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on the provider to notify the Commission under specific conditions.
anchor: the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks
paragraph_text: 1. Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met. That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

object.value: the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met

paragraph_text: 1. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

condition.value: on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems related to products covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I

classification_rationale: This statement clarifies the material scope of Article 60 by ensuring it does not affect other Union or national laws concerning specific high-risk AI systems.
anchor: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems related to products covered by Union harmonisation legislation.
paragraph_text: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems related to products covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I.
parent_texts: 1. Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes may be conducted by providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III, in accordance with this Article and the real-world testing plan referred to in this Article, without prejudice to the prohibitions under Article 5.

object.value: a post-market monitoring system

condition.value: in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system

classification_rationale: This paragraph imposes an obligation on providers to establish and document a post-market monitoring system.
anchor: Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system.

object.value: with the competent authorities
with the notified body concerned

condition.value: during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on the provider to cooperate during investigations.
anchor: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned
span_coverage_truncated: way affect subsequent evaluation causes incident
paragraph_text: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned, during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph, and shall not perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident, prior to informing the competent authorities of such action.
parent_texts: 6. Following the reporting of a serious incident pursuant to paragraph 1, the provider shall, without delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.

object.value: any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned

condition.value: prior to informing the competent authorities of such action

classification_rationale: This statement imposes a prohibition on the provider regarding altering the AI system without prior notification.

span_coverage_truncated: way affect subsequent evaluation causes incident
paragraph_text: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned, during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph, and shall not perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident, prior to informing the competent authorities of such action.
parent_texts: 6. Following the reporting of a serious incident pursuant to paragraph 1, the provider shall, without delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.

subject.value: the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities

object.value: the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV within the premises of those authorities

condition.value: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on the location of technical documentation for specific authorities.
anchor: the technical documentation ... shall remain within the premises of those authorities.
paragraph_text: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III, the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities. Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof. Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation or any copy thereof.
parent_texts: 3. Without prejudice to paragraphs 1 and 2, information exchanged on a confidential basis between the national competent authorities or between national competent authorities and the Commission shall not be disclosed without prior consultation of the originating national competent authority and the deployer when high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III are used by law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities and when such disclosure would jeopardise public and national security interests. This exchange of information shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

This exchange of information shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

anchor: Only staff ... holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation.

paragraph_text: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III, the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities. Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof. Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation or any copy thereof.
parent_texts: 3. Without prejudice to paragraphs 1 and 2, information exchanged on a confidential basis between the national competent authorities or between national competent authorities and the Commission shall not be disclosed without prior consultation of the originating national competent authority and the deployer when high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III are used by law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities and when such disclosure would jeopardise public and national security interests. This exchange of information shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

object.value: clear and meaningful explanations of the role of the AI system in the decision-making procedure and the main elements of the decision taken

condition.value: exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law

classification_rationale: This paragraph establishes an exception to the obligation set out in paragraph 1, making it a deontic statement.
anchor: Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law
paragraph_text: 2. Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law.

condition.value: Depending on the legal system of the Member States, in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States

classification_rationale: This statement grants Member States discretion in how administrative fines are imposed.
anchor: rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies
profile_gate_override: hc=True overridden to False: no MigrainePredict profile dimension matched in the scoped text
paragraph_text: 9. Depending on the legal system of the Member States, the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States. The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.

object.value: an equivalent effect

condition.value: Depending on the legal system of the Member States, the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on Member States regarding the effect of their rules.
anchor: The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.

paragraph_text: 9. Depending on the legal system of the Member States, the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States. The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.

condition.value: intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative dispute resolution

classification_rationale: This statement specifies a condition for AI systems to be classified as high-risk, thus defining part of the material scope.
anchor: AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority
profile_gate_override: hc=False: scope outside the system's operative high-risk basis — keyword present, not the basis; flagged for review
paragraph_text: (a) AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative dispute resolution;
parent_texts: High-risk AI systems pursuant to Article 6(2) are the AI systems listed in any of the following areas:
8. Administration of justice and democratic processes:

object.value: that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17

classification_rationale: This statement describes an obligation for the provider to verify compliance as part of a conformity assessment procedure.
anchor: The provider verifies that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17.
paragraph_text: 2. The provider verifies that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17.
